_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of distributed computing concepts relevant to data processing at scale, including data partitioning strategies, parallel execution models, fault tolerance mechanisms, data locality optimization, and the CAP theorem.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-K-006 (AB, cosine: 0.494), AI-K-019 (AI, cosine: 0.460).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.480). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [microprocessors] vs WIDAI-only terms [cap, computing, concepts, data, distributed, execution] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.886 (P=0.924, R=0.851). P exceeds R by 0.074 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 28 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of microprocessors." and "Knowledge of distributed computing concepts relevant to data processing at scale" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.517 ≈ STS 0.480 (gap 0.036). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [microprocessors]. WIDAI-only: [cap, computing, concepts, data, distributed, execution].
